keyFindings.react: v19.2 stable, React Foundation under Linux Foundation, React Compiler v1.0 stable, CRA deprecated, RSC security patches
keyFindings.nextjs: v16.2 with 400% faster dev startup, AI-first tooling (AGENTS.md, Agent DevTools, browser log forwarding), Turbopack maturing, stable Adapter API for multi-platform
keyFindings.svelte: v5 stable with runes system, new Svelte CLI, SvelteKit with OpenTelemetry, TypeScript 6.0 support
keyFindings.nuxt: MCP toolkit gaining MCP apps support
metaTrends: AI-agent-first development across all frameworks
Rust-based tooling (Turbopack) replacing Webpack
Platform-agnostic deployment (Next.js Adapter API)
Integrated observability (OpenTelemetry in SvelteKit)
Multi-model AI gateways (Vercel AI Gateway: 6+ providers)
Server Components as default rendering paradigm
keyFindings.recommendations: Next.js 16.2+ default for most projects; SvelteKit for performance-critical/simplicity; TypeScript everywhere; Vercel or Cloudflare deployment

keyFindings.bun: v1.3.14 — Bun.Image built-in API, 7x faster warm installs, experimental HTTP/2+HTTP/3 in fetch() and Bun.serve(), FreeBSD/Android builds, Windows ARM64, production-ready runtime
keyFindings.deno: v2.8 — import defer, 6 new subcommands, 3.66x faster npm installs, Deno Deploy GA, Claw Patrol agent firewall open-sourced, Deno Sandbox for untrusted code, better Node.js compat
keyFindings.nodejs: v24.16 LTS / v26.2 Current, stable enterprise choice, native Fetch replacing Axios, OpenJS Foundation governance
keyFindings.edgeComputing: Vercel: 30% of deployments are AI-agent-driven (up 1000%), Cloudflare Workers + D1 + Durable Objects maturing as most complete edge platform, Deno Deploy generally available
keyFindings.vercelAgentic: Three-layer agentic infrastructure model, AI SDK 6 with agent abstraction, Fluid compute for AI workloads, Sandbox for untrusted code, Workflows/Queues for state management
keyFindings.apiPatterns: REST resurging for simplicity, tRPC growing for full-stack TS, SSE > WebSockets when possible, React Server Actions replacing API routes in Next.js
keyFindings.notableEvents: Google Cloud suspended Railway's production account (8hr outage), Azure Linux 4.0 announced, GitHub cuts agent token spend 62% with MCP pruning, Arm open-sources Metis AI security framework
metaTrends: Bun replacing Node.js as default dev/runtime for new projects
Edge computing becoming default deployment target
Agentic infrastructure (30% of deployments are from AI agents)
Security-first runtimes (Deno sandbox, Claw Patrol)
Platform resilience awareness (single-cloud outage risk)
Runtimes converging (Bun/Deno/Node all improve compat)
keyFindings.recommendations: Bun for new projects, Node.js LTS for enterprise, Workers/Deno for edge, multi-cloud strategy for resilience

keyFindings.agenticDeployments: 30% of Vercel deployments now from AI agents (up 1000%), Claude Code 75%, Lovable+v0 6%, Cursor 1.5%. Agent-deployed projects 20x more likely to use AI inference.
keyFindings.agenticInfrastructure: Three-layer model: (1) deployment surfaces for agents, (2) AI primitives (AI SDK 6, AI Gateway, Fluid Compute, Workflows, Sandbox), (3) self-healing infrastructure
keyFindings.inferenceTheft: New attack vector — stolen AI inference resold via OpenAI-compatible adapters. Chipotlai Max targets Chipotle, Home Depot, etc. Per-request bot verification (BotID) required.
keyFindings.vercelWorkflows: GA April 2026, 100M+ runs in beta, 200K npm downloads/week, Python SDK in beta, Workflows 5 coming with concurrency controls and global deployment
keyFindings.claudeCode: Dominant agentic tool (75% share). Andrej Karpathy hired by Anthropic. Opus 4.8 with effort controls, dynamic workflows, better honesty. 'ADHD' experiment shows 2x improvement.
keyFindings.replitIdentity: Visa-backed identity layer for AI agents — agents can now have financial identity
keyFindings.mcp: WebMCP turns any Chrome page into MCP server. DocuSign building agentic agreement MCP. MCP + synthetic data for compliance.
keyFindings.governance: AC/DC framework for governing AI agents. 'CI wasn't built for coding agents.' Agent code explosion requires pipeline rethink.
metaTrends: Agentic infrastructure replacing traditional CI/CD
Claude Code dominant in agentic deployments (75%)
Inference theft as new security threat requiring per-request verification
MCP becoming standard for agent-tool integration
Agent identity and financial rails emerging
Governance and auditability as top constraints
Self-healing infrastructure trending toward autonomous operation
keyFindings.recommendations: Use Claude Code for agentic development, Vercel Workflows for long-running processes, AI Gateway for multi-model AI, per-request bot verification on all AI endpoints, agent-friendly deployment surfaces

keyFindings.vercelChangelog: Two major May 29, 2026 updates: (1) Function invocations now per-unit billing ($0.0000006/inv) for smoother scaling; (2) Docker containers now runnable inside Vercel Sandbox with sudo, FUSE, and VPN support — AI agents can build, test, and verify containerized workloads in isolation
keyFindings.vercelSandbox: Evolved into full system sandbox with Docker, FUSE filesystem drivers, VPN clients, persistent sessions, and system package installation — designed for agentic workloads
keyFindings.netlifyAi: netlify.ai launched April 2026 — first product entry point built exclusively for AI agents, not humans. AX (Agent Experience) is Netlify's framework for agent-first platform design
keyFindings.netlifyAgentRunners: New product: hosted AI agent workloads. Tutorial for building real-time AI chatbots with Agent Runners (no backend code). Competing directly with Vercel Sandbox + Workchains
keyFindings.netlifyDatabase: GA April 28, 2026 — integrated Postgres. Built for AI-native development workflows. Closes gap with Supabase/Neon
keyFindings.cloudflareClaudeAgents: May 19, 2026: Anthropic Claude Managed Agents integrated on Cloudflare — isolated execution for autonomous code delivery, global scaling, customizable tools/runtimes
keyFindings.cloudflareCompliance: Claude Compliance API + Cloudflare CASB integration May 21 — enterprise governance of AI agent activity
keyFindings.railwayOutage: Google Cloud suspended Railway's production account causing 8-hour platform-wide outage (InfoQ, May 30) — single-cloud dependency is a critical risk
keyFindings.openrouterFunding: $113M Series B raised — validates multi-model AI gateway pattern (single endpoint, hundreds of models)
keyFindings.githubCostControl: Agent workflow token spend cut 62% with daily audits + MCP pruning — governance and cost control critical at scale
metaTrends: Agent-first platforms: Vercel, Netlify, Cloudflare all building for AI agents as primary users
Docker/containerization becoming native platform capability (Vercel Docker-in-Sandbox, Cloudflare Containers)
Single-cloud dependency risk proven by Railway outage — multi-cloud resilience now essential
Unified AI primitives: deployment + inference + database + agent hosting in single platforms
Agent governance at scale: token auditing, compliance APIs, per-request verification as mandatory
AX (Agent Experience) emerging as new product design paradigm — platforms must serve machine consumers
Edge + AI merge: Claude agents on Cloudflare edge, Vercel Edge compute, Netlify Functions
Pricing model shift: per-unit billing (Vercel), per-build over per-seat (Netlify) to match agent-scale usage
keyFindings.recommendations: Vercel for full-stack Next.js with Docker-in-Sandbox testing; Netlify Agent Runners for hosted AI agents; Cloudflare Workers for edge API + Claude agent hosting; multi-cloud design from day one; per-unit function pricing; agent governance and token auditing from the start

coveredTopics: GitHub agent token cost control (62% reduction)
